title: Auto-update policy not working with private Harbor registry
body:
### Issue Description

I am experiencing an issue where the Podman auto-update policy is not functioning correctly with a private Harbor registry. Here are the details of my setup and the problem:

- I can successfully log in to the Harbor registry and perform push/pull operations for images.
- However, the auto-update mechanism does not update the running containers. The containers  are configured as systemd services using quadlets.
- I have set the `AutoUpdate=registry` directive in the quadlet files.

I've performed a dry run with the following command: `podman auto-update --dry-run --format "{{.Image}} {{.Updated}}"`. The output consistently shows `Updated: false`. 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6039ca64-fc0d-416e-bfd8-6a4e57dd9208)

As you can see, there is one newer Image with a different digest:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f8358f2a-c0c9-4528-bce4-b7c97c254fdd)


### Steps to reproduce the issue

See Description


### Describe the results you received

The auto-update feature does not detect updates and leaves the containers unchanged.

### Describe the results you expected

Containers running with `AutoUpdate=registry` should detect updated images in the Harbor registry and update automatically

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Same issue here, I just hosted Harbor on my homelab and my containers are no longer auto updating... I'll have to do it manually again, a shame
